steps.title: Identify the structure needing punctuation
steps.content: The sentence reads wildlife biologist [blank] is on the front lines. The blank requires a name that functions as an appositive—a noun phrase identifying or renaming “wildlife biologist.”

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule
steps.content:
Appositives require either no punctuation (restrictive appositives, where the name is essential to identify the noun) or paired commas (non-restrictive appositives, where the name provides extra information).

Since “wildlife biologist” doesn't uniquely identify a person, “Dr.

Purnima Devi Barman” is restrictive and must have no punctuation between the noun and name.

The other three incorrectly introduce punctuation (B's colon, C's paired commas) or violate punctuation rules (D's single comma, which requires both commas or none).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
